standard_ai.name: Standard AI prompt
standard_ai.definition: Natural-language instructions given to a repo-aware AI. The AI may inspect, reason, edit, or return local commands, but the prompt is not itself a Goodnight, Have Fun launch command.

gnhf.name: Goodnight, Have Fun (GNHF) prompt
gnhf.definition: A bounded PowerShell terminal launch command beginning with gnhf and carrying explicit agent, Git mode, iteration, token, sleep, and stop contracts.

new_prompts.sprint_path_role: Resolve and verify the local repository directory before emitting repository commands
new_prompts.use_this_when: The repository URL or name is known but the operator's exact local directory is unknown, stale, or easy to confuse with another checkout.
new_prompts.inspect_first: Repo URL/name, current shell, current directory, git worktrees, common development roots, repo remotes, and any path already supplied by the operator.
new_prompts.expected_output: One verified repository root, shell-matched Set-Location or cd command, root verification, branch/worktree evidence, and only then the requested commands.
new_prompts.next_step: Use P46 for zero-token local tests, P47 for factoring analysis, or the task-specific sequential prompt suite after the directory gate passes.
new_prompts.proof_gate: The first executable repository command changes to the verified root and git rev-parse --show-toplevel resolves to that same directory.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) TERMINAL COMMAND.
ANALYZE THE LOCAL REPOSITORY LOCATION BEFORE GIVING THE USER ANY REPOSITORY COMMANDS.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Known local path, if any: xyz_known_or_unknown
Requested task: xyz_task
DIRECTORY GATE
1. Inspect the available local-shell and Git evidence. Do not assume that the current directory, repository name, Desktop path, or remembered path is correct.
2. Resolve the intended checkout by matching its Git remote to the requested repository and by inspecting git worktree list when available.
3. When the root is already reachable, verify it with git rev-parse --show-toplevel and git remote get-url origin.
4. When the path cannot yet be proven, give only bounded discovery commands. Do not give tests, builds, mutations, commits, or deployment commands from an unverified directory.
5. After resolving the root, make the first executable task command a shell-matched directory change: Set-Location -LiteralPath "<verified-root>" for PowerShell or cd -- "<verified-root>" for Bash.
6. Immediately verify that the active root equals the resolved root before any later command.
HARNESS CONTEXT
Name repo, branch or worktree, PR or sprint, lane, owned scope, forbidden scope, expected artifacts, validation order, proof level, and proof ceiling.
Search existing contracts, helpers, validators, scripts, manifests, and output patterns before inventing.
FINAL RESPONSE
Return the verified root and evidence, the exact directory-change command, the root-verification command, branch/worktree state, and then the bounded task commands in execution order.
Never place commands that act on the repository before the directory gate.

new_prompts.sprint_path_role: Analyze the repository and give the operator exact local tests that consume no model or provider tokens
new_prompts.use_this_when: The operator has no model, API, provider, or coding-agent tokens but can run local repository tools.
new_prompts.inspect_first: Verified repo root, repo instructions, package manifests, test runners, validators, scripts, CI workflows, language versions, fixtures, changed files, and generated-output policy.
new_prompts.expected_output: Directory-safe local commands for syntax, focused tests, validators, broader tests, artifact checks, and Git evidence, with no token-consuming agent launch.
new_prompts.next_step: Run the commands locally, preserve the output, and use P49 to interpret results and close out or choose the next repair.
new_prompts.proof_gate: Every command is local, begins after the verified directory change, maps to repository evidence, and requires no model, API, provider, or agent tokens.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) TERMINAL COMMAND.
ANALYZE THE REPOSITORY AND GIVE THE USER LOCAL TESTS FOR A ZERO-TOKEN SITUATION.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Verified local root or directory evidence: xyz_path_or_evidence
Changed surface or intended task: xyz_scope
DIRECTORY GATE

Resolve and verify the repository root before returning any test, validator, build, Git, or artifact command. The first executable command must enter that exact root, followed by git rev-parse --show-toplevel.
ZERO-TOKEN BOUNDARY
Do not launch GNHF, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor agents, Goose, AGY, hosted models, local models, or any command that consumes model, API, provider, or coding-agent tokens.
Do not replace executable repository tests with generic advice.
ANALYSIS
1. Read repo instructions and identify the actual language, package manager, test runner, validator CLIs, build scripts, workflow files, and changed-file scope.
2. Reuse repository-owned commands and narrow tests before broad tests.
3. Identify commands that are safe offline and mark network, credentials, Office UI, browser, hardware, deployment, or production checks as separate blocked or operator gates.
4. Never invent a test script, target, package command, or path that is not supported by repository evidence.
OUTPUT ORDER
1. directory change and root verification
2. environment and dependency presence checks that do not install anything
3. parse or syntax checks
4. focused unit or contract tests
5. repository validators and artifact checks
6. broader tests only when justified
7. git diff --check, git status --short, and generated-output or secret hygiene
8. exact expected success evidence and interpretation of common failures
Report repo, branch/worktree, sprint, lane, owned and forbidden scope, expected artifacts, exact commands, skipped checks, proof achievable locally, proof ceiling, and one next decision.

new_prompts.sprint_path_role: Analyze whether repository responsibilities are correctly factored before assigning implementation
new_prompts.use_this_when: The repository may mix harness, agent routing, application logic, integration seams, validation, runtime proof, and docs, or the factoring state is unknown.
new_prompts.inspect_first: Verified repo root, instructions, codebase map, manifests, workflows, skills, capabilities, triggers, domain modules, scripts, validators, tests, reports, open PRs, and recent diffs.
new_prompts.expected_output: An evidence-backed factoring map, collision ledger, keep/create/split/merge/retire/rewire decisions, and bounded implementation sprints for every real gap.
new_prompts.next_step: Use P48 only for the selected factoring build; do not substitute the analysis for required implementation.
new_prompts.proof_gate: Every factoring claim names repository evidence, every gap has an owner, and harness work is separated from application-logic work when file ownership would collide.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) TERMINAL COMMAND.
ANALYZE AND FACTOR THE REPOSITORY. DO NOT ASSUME THE FACTORING SURFACES ALREADY EXIST.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Verified local root or directory evidence: xyz_path_or_evidence
Requested product or harness concern: xyz_concern
DIRECTORY GATE
Verify the repository root before giving repository commands or file-path conclusions.
FACTORING SURFACES

Classify every material responsibility into one primary surface: harness spine; agent harness; conventional application logic; integration seam; docs/reporting; validation; runtime proof; research/design.
For skills, capabilities, and triggers decide KEEP, CREATE, SPLIT, MERGE, RETIRE, or REWIRE.

Skills contain reusable workflow guidance. Capabilities expose reusable operations. Triggers deterministically route conditions. Product behavior remains in code and domain contracts rather than hidden in prompts.
EVIDENCE LOOP
1. Inspect repo rules, Git state, worktrees, recent commits, open PRs, maps, manifests, scripts, validators, tests, generated-output policy, and current artifacts.
2. Search existing names and owners before inventing.
3. Name the current owner, activation condition, inputs, outputs, preconditions, forbidden conditions, guardrails, tests, proof level, and likely files for each reusable surface.
4. Identify duplicate owners, hidden prompt-only product behavior, stale registries, unassigned harness gaps, oversized modules, cross-layer imports, and validation blind spots.
5. Separate analysis-only unknowns from buildable gaps. Research is allowed only when a real unknown blocks safe construction.
6. Assign every buildable gap to a bounded implementation sprint and identify safe parallel lanes and collision files.
OUTPUT

Return current state, factoring map, decisions, collision ledger, missing or stale surfaces, exact files to inspect first, local zero-token validation commands, dependency order, implementation owners, proof ceiling, and one exact next decision.
A factoring gap with no build owner is an incomplete result.

new_prompts.sprint_path_role: Implement one bounded factoring sprint after evidence identifies a real repository gap
new_prompts.use_this_when: P47 or equivalent repository evidence has identified one bounded harness, agent-harness, application, seam, validation, or docs factoring gap.
new_prompts.inspect_first: Verified root, selected factoring ledger row, governing repo rules, current owner files, tests, validators, dependent PRs, and collision boundaries.
new_prompts.expected_output: Tracked implementation, updated contracts and tests, local validation evidence, coherent commit, pushed branch, and opened or updated PR when available.
new_prompts.next_step: Use P49 for local validation and closeout, then return to the sequential prompt suite for cleanup, merge, release, or final closeout.
new_prompts.proof_gate: The selected factoring gap is implemented in repository files and contracts; a plan-only response or prompt-only product behavior is failure.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) TERMINAL COMMAND.
IMPLEMENT THE SELECTED REPOSITORY FACTORING SPRINT. DO NOT RETURN A FACTORING MAP OR PLAN ONLY.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Verified local root or directory evidence: xyz_path_or_evidence
Selected factoring gap: xyz_selected_gap
Branch or worktree: xyz_branch_or_worktree
PR or sprint: xyz_pr_or_sprint
Lane: xyz_lane
Owned scope: xyz_owned_scope
Forbidden scope: xyz_forbidden_scope
Expected artifacts: xyz_expected_artifacts
Validation order: xyz_validation_order
DIRECTORY GATE
Resolve, enter, and verify the exact repository root before any test, mutation, Git, or GitHub command.
EXECUTION
1. Inspect existing owners, contracts, helpers, validators, scripts, manifests, tests, logs, reports, branch conventions, and generated-output policy.
2. Preserve useful work and use an isolated worktree when the current checkout contains unrelated changes.
3. Implement the smallest coherent factoring change in the correct surface. Keep application behavior in code and domain contracts, not only in prompts.
4. Update every connected operational artifact required by the change: schemas, fixtures, skills, capabilities, triggers, workflows, scripts, validators, docs, reports, or manifests.
5. Add focused executable tests that fail before the repair and pass after it.
6. Run the specified validation order, then git diff --check, generated-output and secret hygiene, and git status --short.
7. Commit coherent changes. Push normally and open or update the intended PR when the environment allows.
8. Do not merge, deploy, force-push, delete unknown worktrees, weaken validators, or broaden into forbidden scope.
FINAL RESPONSE

Report repo, root, branch/worktree, PR/sprint, lane, files, artifacts, validations, commit SHA, push and PR state, skipped checks, gaps, proof level, proof ceiling, final Git state, and one exact next command.

new_prompts.sprint_path_role: Interpret local tests, verify Git evidence, and choose the next bounded decision without consuming agent tokens
new_prompts.use_this_when: Local commands have been run and the operator needs an evidence-based closeout, blocker report, or next command without launching another model-backed agent.
new_prompts.inspect_first: Verified root, command output, test logs, validator reports, generated artifacts, git diff/status/log, branch/worktree state, push state, and PR state if available.
new_prompts.expected_output: Evidence classification, completed work, exact failures, proof level and ceiling, Git state, and one safe next local command or task-specific prompt selection.
new_prompts.next_step: Use P06 for cleanup, P14 for a broken PR, P15 for merge/release, or P12 for final closeout only when their entry conditions are satisfied.
new_prompts.proof_gate: No completion claim is made without passing checks or exact artifact/Git evidence, and no runtime or browser proof is inferred from static tests.
lines: PROMPT SURFACE: STANDARD AI. THIS IS NOT A GOODNIGHT, HAVE FUN (GNHF) TERMINAL COMMAND.
ANALYZE THE LOCAL RESULTS AND CLOSE OUT THE BOUNDED SPRINT WITHOUT LAUNCHING A TOKEN-CONSUMING AGENT.
Repository: xyz_repo_url_or_name
Verified local root or directory evidence: xyz_path_or_evidence
Commands and outputs: xyz_local_evidence
Intended sprint: xyz_sprint
DIRECTORY GATE

Before giving any follow-up command, verify that the command will run from the same proven repository root. Put the exact Set-Location or cd command first when the shell is not already proven to be there.
EVIDENCE REVIEW
1. Separate PASS, FAIL, BLOCKED, NOT RUN, and NOT APPLICABLE. Never convert missing evidence into PASS.
2. Map each result to the exact test, validator, artifact, file, commit, branch, worktree, push, PR, release, deployment, or runtime claim it can support.
3. Distinguish static, build, CI, local runtime, browser, Office, deployment, and operator-acceptance proof.
4. Inspect git diff --check, git status --short, git log --oneline --decorate -5, and generated-output or secret hygiene when those outputs are available.
5. Identify whether the next route is P03 unknown repo intake, P06 cleanup, P07 implementation, P14 broken PR repair, P15 merge/release, P20 selected opportunity, or P12 final closeout.
OUTPUT

Return completed work, exact validation evidence, artifacts and logs, failures and blockers, skipped checks, risks, proof achieved, proof ceiling, branch/worktree and PR state, and one exact directory-safe next command.
Do not claim commit, push, PR mutation, merge, release, deployment, or runtime behavior without direct evidence.
